column2: A class defines the structure and behavior of objects, including fields, methods, and constructors, serving as a template for object instantiation. Variants include concrete, abstract, utility, final, nested, and exception classes.
column3: exception class, private static nested class, checked exception class, abstract class, concrete class, final class, utility class

column2: An interface defines a contract of method signatures that implementing classes must fulfill. Variants include regular interfaces, functional interfaces, annotation interfaces, nested interfaces, and concurrent interfaces.
column3: nested interface, nested interface type, interface type, annotation interface, functional interface, concurrent interface

column2: A method is a callable block of code that performs a specific operation, defined within a class or interface. Variants include instance methods, static methods, and abstract methods.
column3: instance method, abstract method, static method

column2: A package is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces for organization and access control.
